# Nanohertz GWB Bayesian Model Comparison

> Authoritative paper, evidence results, public data, and code for Bayesian source-model comparison of the NANOGrav 15-year nanohertz gravitational-wave background.

## Paper

- [Publication hub](https://hkust-aaron.github.io/nanohertz-gwb-model-comparison-repro/): Direct summary, evidence table, applications, FAQ, citation, and resource links.
- [Published article](https://doi.org/10.3390/sym18071169): Version of Record in Symmetry 18 (2026), article 1169.
- [OpenAlex record](https://openalex.org/W7167924336): Open scholarly graph record for the article.
- [Semantic Scholar record](https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/56225036fbd4d9a05f81c574e4f144a595306aa3): Semantic Scholar article record.
- [Open PDF](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/HKUST-AARON/nanohertz-gwb-model-comparison-repro/main/paper/Xu_Zhang_Guo_2026_Symmetry_VOR.pdf): Full 28-page published Version of Record under CC BY 4.0.
- [Graphical abstract](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/HKUST-AARON/nanohertz-gwb-model-comparison-repro/main/assets/graphical-abstract-nanohertz-gwb.jpg): Visual explanation from Hellings-Downs angular symmetry to physical source models.
- [BibTeX citation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/HKUST-AARON/nanohertz-gwb-model-comparison-repro/main/CITATION.bib): Canonical BibTeX record.
- [RIS citation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/HKUST-AARON/nanohertz-gwb-model-comparison-repro/main/CITATION.ris): Canonical RIS record.
- [CSL-JSON citation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/HKUST-AARON/nanohertz-gwb-model-comparison-repro/main/CITATION.json): Canonical CSL-JSON record.

## Data and Code

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/HKUST-AARON/nanohertz-gwb-model-comparison-repro): Public analysis scripts, numerical outputs, posterior samples, paper, and citation metadata.
- [CC BY 4.0 license](https://github.com/HKUST-AARON/nanohertz-gwb-model-comparison-repro/blob/main/LICENSE): Reuse terms for repository-authored materials.
- [Zenodo data archive](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20319210): Data-only reproducibility archive with a persistent DOI.
- [Machine-readable model comparison](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/HKUST-AARON/nanohertz-gwb-model-comparison-repro/main/analysis_outputs/kde_model_comparison/model_comparison.json): Delta log evidence and Bayes factors for five spectral models.
- [Reproducibility workflow](https://github.com/HKUST-AARON/nanohertz-gwb-model-comparison-repro/blob/main/repro/README.md): Environment, public inputs, commands, and method details.

## Key Facts

- Title: Bayesian Evidence for Angular Symmetry and Spectral Curvature in the Nanohertz Gravitational-Wave Background
- Authors: Hua Xu, Weiming Zhang, Yike Guo
- Journal: Symmetry, volume 18, issue 7, article 1169
- Published: 10 July 2026
- Article DOI: 10.3390/sym18071169
- Data DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20319210
- Article-cited archive DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20581821
- License: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0)
- Primary input: corrected NANOGrav 15-year Hellings-Downs free-spectrum KDE, version 1.1.0
- Main result: spectra with curvature or a characteristic scale are favored over the canonical scale-free SMBHB spectrum
- Leading physical template: first-order phase transition under the baseline priors
- Leading astrophysical model: environmental SMBHB hardening

## Extended Context

- [Full publication context](https://hkust-aaron.github.io/nanohertz-gwb-model-comparison-repro/llms-full.txt): Detailed findings, numerical evidence, scope, applications, and citation.
